message:
feat(polyfills): Add native crypto module (hash, hmac, aes-gcm)

Moves cryptographic operations from host functions into native Zig code
running in WASM sandbox using std.crypto. This eliminates unnecessary
sandbox boundary crossings for crypto operations.

Implemented functions:
- hash(algorithm, data) - SHA-256, SHA-512, SHA-1
- hmac(algorithm, key, data) - HMAC-SHA256, HMAC-SHA512
- aesGcmEncrypt(key, iv, plaintext, aad) - AES-256-GCM encryption
- aesGcmDecrypt(key, iv, ciphertext, aad) - AES-256-GCM decryption

Only random_bytes remains as a host function (for CSPRNG).
All crypto operations use stack buffers (64KB) for performance.

This reduces the crypto host function surface from 5 functions down to 1:
- ❌ Removed: __edgebox_hash (use crypto.hash)
- ❌ Removed: __edgebox_hmac (use crypto.hmac)
- ❌ Removed: __edgebox_aes_gcm_encrypt (use crypto.aesGcmEncrypt)
- ❌ Removed: __edgebox_aes_gcm_decrypt (use crypto.aesGcmDecrypt)
- ✅ Keep: __edgebox_random_bytes (true host function)
